text: Git branching is a powerful feature in version control that helps teams work together without conflicts. When you start a new feature, you create a separate feature branch from the main codebase. This allows you to make changes, test new code, and experiment without affecting the stable version. Once your work is complete, you can merge your branch back into the main branch. However, before merging, most teams use pull requests. A pull request is a request to review your changes before they are added. This creates a safe, organized workflow where each new feature is developed, reviewed, and merged step by step.
